questions.question: What is the value of (+5) + (-3)?
questions.explanation: The tokens cancel each other out, leaving 2 positives.

questions.question: How many zero pairs are needed to subtract (+10) - (+7)?
questions.explanation: To take away 7 positives from 10, we need 3 zero pairs.

questions.question: What is the result of (-8) + (+4)?
questions.explanation: The negatives and positives cancel each other out, leaving -4.

questions.question: How many tokens are left after subtracting (-5) - (+3)?
questions.explanation: We take away 3 positives from 5, leaving 2 negatives and 1 positive.

questions.question: What is the initial bank balance if you deposit ₹100?
questions.explanation: The account starts with a balance of ₹100.

questions.question: What is the new bank balance after depositing ₹60?
questions.explanation: We add ₹60 to the initial balance of ₹100.

questions.question: What happens when you pay an electric bill of ₹30?
questions.explanation: You subtract ₹30 from your current bank balance.

questions.question: What is the new bank balance after paying an electric bill of ₹30?
questions.explanation: We subtract ₹30 from the previous balance of ₹160.

questions.question: Is it possible for a bank account to have a negative balance?
questions.explanation: Some banks allow accounts to become negative temporarily, with additional fees or interest.

questions.question: What happens when you make a major purchase of ₹150?
questions.explanation: You subtract ₹150 from your current bank balance.

important_notes.title: Tokens and Zero Pairs
important_notes.content: A positive and negative token cancel each other out, forming a zero pair. We can use these to simplify calculations with integers.

important_notes.title: Subtraction using Tokens
important_notes.content: To subtract an integer, we add its inverse instead. This allows us to use tokens to visualize the process.

important_notes.title: Bank Account Balances
important_notes.content: A bank account balance can be positive or negative, depending on deposits and withdrawals. Some banks charge additional fees for negative balances.

important_notes.title: Integers in Real-World Applications
important_notes.content: Integers are used to represent quantities with no units, such as temperatures, heights, and times. They are also used in financial transactions like bank account balances.

important_notes.title: Token Model for Addition
important_notes.content: We can use tokens to visualize the process of adding integers. A positive token represents an addition operation, while a negative token represents a subtraction operation.

facts.fact: Integers were first introduced by Indian mathematician Aryabhata in the 5th century AD.
The concept of zero as a number was developed independently in ancient India and China around the same time.
The token model for addition is based on the idea that positive tokens represent additions, while negative tokens represent subtractions.
Integers are used extensively in computer science, particularly in algorithms and data structures.
The concept of bank account balances was first introduced by medieval merchants to keep track of transactions.
